Non-goals (YAGNI): dropping `--instrument`, renaming the JSONL path, `src/ledger.rs`, `src/table.rs`, a generated round table, a new log record type.

Outcome (complete; three increments). Increment A (`risk_class` required in `src/metrics.rs` + backfill into the 46 historical JSONL records, 40 low-risk / 6 risky grounded in the ledger; merged to main at `fc7f2b6`): built by an isolated implementer in worktree `impl/round-log-core`, converged low-risk in two review-in-worktree rounds (round 1 fixed the `instrument.md` optional-vs-required mismatch both reviewers flagged; round 2 clean; no misclassification confirmed). Increment B (the core reshape, merged at `1fada25`): removed the ledger template's `## Round summaries` + `## Findings` tables and the `Artifact classification` line; rewrote convergence accounting in `pack/AGENTS.md` + `pack/prompts/orchestrator.md` so the orchestrator counts the streak and total-round count from the always-present ledger NARRATIVE (Q-1 substance unchanged, only the source moved from the table); framed the JSONL as the opt-in structured superset the orchestrator ALSO appends when instrumented (opt-in preserved throughout, the explorers' always-on recommendation was declined by the human); and folded in the plan/ledger DE-DUP rule (the plan owns step status via the Roadmap and decisions via Open Questions; the ledger references the plan; RESUME STATE is a pointer plus transient state, not a Roadmap restatement). Classified RISKY (rewrites the core convergence rule) and converged in two consecutive clean rounds after the round-1 fixes (the high finding was the template's own RESUME STATE comment still telling authors to restate status). Increment C (orchestrator-owned): slimmed the live `docs/plans/agent-scaffold.ledger.md` (removed the two stale tables, rewrote the RESUME STATE to a pointer + transient per the de-dup rule, dropped the pack-rule restatements) and cleaned the live guidance in this plan (the `workflow-calibration` collection method). NOTE: several COMPLETE steps' historical Outcome/scope narratives (`convergence-accounting`, `state-schema`, `instrument-flag`) still mention the ledger round-summary table; those are frozen historical records of what each step did at the time and are superseded by this migration (counting is now from the narrative; structured round data lives only in the round log). 96 tests, clippy clean, scaffold byte-identical, `validate` clean throughout. Rounds logged to `docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl` (records 53-57). Deferred to `workflow-invariants`: the `trivial`-completion mechanism and the grandfather boundary (including the six pre-logging tasks that appear in the log but never reach a clean-round streak). See `docs/plans/agent-scaffold.ledger.md` for the round records.

### `ledger-parse`: superseded by `round-log-core` (`skipped`)

Skipped. Its premise (parse the ledger's round-summary + a formalised register table, add `src/ledger.rs` and a shared `src/table.rs`, PoC a rich ledger parse) evaporated once `round-log-core` (`Q-34`) removed the ledger's structured tables and made the JSONL the single structured store. The only residual, extracting the `## RESUME STATE` section verbatim for `status --resume`, is a ~10-line section extractor that folds into `state-queries`. No `src/ledger.rs`, no `src/table.rs` (the `plan.rs` pipe-table helper has no second consumer, so it is not extracted).

### `workflow-invariants`: `validate --workflow` enforcement (`Q-27`)

Next. Decided (`Q-27`, human, after a two-independent-explorer design pass synthesised by the orchestrator; design record in `docs/plans/workflow-invariants.explorations/trivial-and-grandfather-{A,B}.md`); re-scoped by `round-log-core` (`Q-34`) to read the JSONL round log directly, not a ledger parse (the `ledger-parse` keystone was SKIPPED; there is no `src/ledger.rs`). Extend `validate` with a `--workflow` mode that cross-references plan status against the JSONL round log (`docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl`), hard-failing (exit 1) into the existing `validate` problem list. Detection not prevention: runs in CI, the checks pre-commit hook, or on demand, with no binary required at workflow runtime; it is an opt-in-logging feature (meaningful when instrumentation is on).

The key invariant (W3): for every Roadmap step marked `complete`, filter `round` records whose `task` leading-slug (before an optional `-inc<x>` suffix) equals the step slug, then GROUP those records BY INCREMENT (the leading slug plus its optional `-inc<x>` suffix); within each increment the `risk_class` must be consistent and the consecutive-clean streak (the peak over that increment's records, since `consecutive_clean` is a per-loop running streak that spans rounds naming different artifacts) must reach the required count for that class (`low_risk` 1, `risky` 2). W3 checks ONLY `complete` steps. A `complete` step with no matching round records is the `pause.md` catch, UNLESS it carries one of the two review-exempt statuses below.

The two owed sub-decisions are now settled (both decided by the human in the design pass):

- (A) A declared trivial completion uses a new `trivial` Roadmap status in `plan.rs` (added to `ROADMAP_STATUSES`, so the plan-template drift guard covers it): a `trivial` step declares a review-skipped completion, single-sourced in the status column, drift-guard-covered, and the hardest of the candidates to fake (chosen over a log record for a round that never happened). W3 does not require rounds for a `trivial` step, so a silent skip becomes a visible, declared one.
- (B) A `grandfathered` Roadmap status (a parallel terminal status in `plan.rs`) exempts pre-logging legacy steps from W3. Chosen over a positional cutoff (brittle to future insertion, repo-specific) and a closed slug list (a separate artifact that drifts): a status is coherent with the `trivial` choice, insertion-safe, and general for any project. The earliest steps that predate logging are relabelled `grandfathered` when this lands.

BUILD-TIME MUSTS both explorers surfaced (get these wrong and `round-log-core` false-flags itself): the leading-slug strip must handle an ALPHANUMERIC `-inc<x>` suffix (`round-log-core` logs its increments as `-incA` / `-incB`, not `-inc1` / `-inc2`), and the streak / risk_class-consistency checks must run PER INCREMENT, not per step slug (increment A converged `low_risk` at streak 1 while increment B converged `risky` at streak 2, so a single-step aggregate would see an inconsistent `risk_class` and a broken streak).

Data grounding the grandfather boundary (from the `round-log-core` increment-A backfill review, 2026-07-16): the only real complete STEPS whose backfilled rounds never reach the required streak are the three b2 short-streak steps `convergence-accounting`, `pack-rebuild-tracking`, and `user-prompts-dir` (they converged informally before per-round logging was disciplined). Eleven b1 steps (`core-assets` .. `init-vcs`) have ZERO round records (they predate logging entirely). Other never-converging log tasks (`workflow-hardening`, `plan-maintenance`, `consolidate-plan`, and similar review tasks) are ORPHANS with no Roadmap row at all, so W3 never checks them. The `grandfathered` status covers the b1 and b2 real steps; the orphans need no handling because W3 iterates Roadmap steps, not log tasks.

Also move the round-log internal-consistency check here (a `clean` record increments `consecutive_clean`, a `new_valid` resets it). NOTE (drift-guard gap, from the same review): the metrics drift guard checks that schema field NAMES appear in the prose but not their required-vs-optional STATUS (which let the increment-A `risk_class` required/optional mismatch slip to review); consider strengthening it here or in a later hardening step (not auto-in-scope). Explicitly rejected: the `agent-scaffold step complete <slug>` state-machine mutation gate (prevention, stateful, a second plan writer, a runtime dependency); detection gives the same guarantee without those costs. Depends on `round-log-core`; reads `src/plan.rs` + `src/metrics.rs` (no new parser, no `src/ledger.rs`).

### `escalation-exempt`: W3 accepts a human-authorized below-streak convergence (`Q-40`)

Deferred, folded into `Q-44`. This step would add a THIRD W3-exemption special-case (`escalation` acceptance) alongside `grandfathered` and `trivial`. The human's 2026-07-18 architecture pivot (Project Principle 8) and cleanup mandate identified exemption proliferation as a sharp edge to UNIFY rather than extend, so do NOT build this as a one-off; its need (representing a human-authorised below-streak convergence so W3 does not false-flag it) is carried into `Q-44`'s exemption-unification design, and the approach recorded below is the input to that, not a standalone build. Original coupling note (still true): it reads the existing `type:"escalation"` record, which the receipt-encoding passes confirmed is independent of the decision-receipt mechanism. Decided (`Q-40`, human, 2026-07-18): the ESCALATION-RECORD-AWARE approach. Problem: `optional-modules` increment 2c-ii was `risky` (W3 requires peak `consecutive_clean` >= 2) but the human accepted it at ONE clean round via the escalation contract at the 5-round total-cap (the write-escape class was closed and independently confirmed). Its round records therefore show risky/peak-clean-1, which W3 will false-flag as non-converged once `optional-modules` is marked `complete`. Neither `trivial` (review was not skipped) nor `grandfathered` (it is not pre-logging) fits.

The decided fix: W3, when an increment's peak `consecutive_clean` falls short of its `risk_class` requirement, does NOT flag it if that increment's records are accompanied by a `type:"escalation"` record with `human_decision:"decision"` (matched by the increment's `task`); that record is the durable evidence the human authorized convergence, so W3 treats the increment as converged. Chosen over a new Roadmap override status/marker (new vocabulary + a drift surface where the marker can disagree with the log) and over a new accepted-risk annotation field (new schema for what the escalation record already implies), because it reuses data ALREADY in the log (the `optional-modules-inc2cii` escalation record is present, record 83), adds no new schema or status, and ties the exemption precisely to the decision that authorized it (auditable). Principle-grounded: Principle 16 (one source of truth, the escalation record is the single authority for the decision) and Principle 2 (minimal, no new machinery).

Build spec: in `src/workflow.rs`, the per-increment convergence check gains an escalation-exemption branch. Precise semantics: (a) the exemption fires ONLY on `human_decision:"decision"`, never `"resume"` (a resume is not an acceptance); (b) it is scoped to the one increment whose `task` matches the escalation record, never a blanket bypass; (c) it exempts ONLY the clean-streak shortfall, the `risk_class`-consistency check and every other W3 check still apply; (d) a `complete` step whose increment converged this way passes W3 with an informational note rather than an error. Tests: an increment with risky/peak-clean-1 records PLUS an escalation-decision record passes; the same records WITHOUT the escalation record still flag; the same records with a `"resume"` escalation still flag; risk_class inconsistency still flags even with an escalation record. Dogfood acceptance: after this lands, marking `optional-modules` `complete` and running `validate --workflow` on this repo's own plan exits 0. Risk note for review: this makes W3 trust the escalation record as authority; that is intended (the record IS the human decision), but reviewers should probe that the exemption cannot be widened accidentally (scoping (a)-(c)). Classified RISKY at loop-open (it edits the enforcement backstop that guards workflow integrity, the same load-bearing surface `workflow-invariants` built) -> two consecutive clean rounds. Depends on `workflow-invariants`; reads `src/workflow.rs` + `src/metrics.rs` (the escalation record type already exists and validates).

### `decision-receipt`: the decision-receipt record + W4 (Q-44 pilot 1)

Next. The first `Q-44` structured-data pilot (human-decided 2026-07-18: build the pilots now, decide the skeleton depth after). Resolves `Q-42` (build a Full receipt) and `Q-43` (encoding) as the converged substrate, placed as the first instance of the structured-data architecture. Design record: `docs/plans/receipt-encoding.explorations/receipt-encoding-{A,B}.md` (the substrate) and `docs/plans/structured-architecture.explorations/target-arch-{A,B,C}.md` (all three place it identically as pilot 1). Scope: a `type:"decision"` JSONL record in `docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl` with `q_id`, `options` (non-empty array of strings), `recommendation`, `chosen` (validated as a member of `options`, the one genuinely new cross-field constraint), and optional `ts`; a `metrics::parse_decisions` best-effort projection parallel to `parse_rounds`; the metrics drift-guard extended (add the `decision` type and its fields to `pack/instrument.md` and the guard); and a W4 `validate --workflow` check that every `decided -> folded into <slug>` Open-Questions item has a matching `q_id` receipt. W4 is FORWARD-LOOKING and must NOT flag the ~40 historical decided items (Q-1..Q-44) that predate the mechanism. CORRECTED (round-1 review, high finding V1): the boundary must NOT be derived from the receipt set. Any receipt-derived boundary (min or max `q_id` among existing receipts) is CIRCULAR, the quantity W4 checks (is a receipt missing?) is the same quantity that would set the boundary, so a forgotten receipt moves its own exemption boundary and slips through (a demonstrated silent false-pass). The boundary must be an INDEPENDENT DECLARED cutoff: this step pulls the `baseline` primitive forward (it was earmarked for `waiver-model` as the shared W3/W4 historical-exemption mechanism). Add a `type:"baseline"` record carrying a decided-question cutoff (for example `questions_through: "Q-44"`, meaning Q-1..Q-44 predate the mechanism and are exempt); W4 requires a receipt for a decided item iff its `q_id` index is STRICTLY GREATER than the baseline's cutoff index. Semantics when NO baseline record exists: W4 requires a receipt for EVERY decided item (exemption must be DECLARED and visible, matching the pause.md-catch ethos; a fresh scaffolded project has no pre-existing decided items so needs no baseline, and every decision it makes under the active mechanism correctly requires a receipt; only a repo migrating with pre-existing decisions declares a baseline to exempt them). This step's migration WRITES ONE `type:"baseline"` record to the real log declaring `questions_through: "Q-44"`. Design the `type:"baseline"` record to be extensible so `waiver-model` can add a steps/rounds cutoff for W3's historical exemption to the same record type. Also adopt the convention (pack docs) that a `decided` item's prose need not re-enumerate the option labels (the receipt is their machine home). `src/plan.rs` and the plan-template stay byte-identical (the receipt lives in the JSONL event log, the skeleton decision is deferred). Preserve the strict-validate vs best-effort-projection split (`parse_decisions` drops malformed lines; `validate_log` reports them; both run). Classify RISKY (it extends the enforcement backstop like `workflow-invariants`/W3) -> two consecutive clean rounds. Dogfood: once built, `Q-44` itself and future decided items get a `type:"decision"` record. Verification: `just test`/`just clippy`/`nix fmt`, ASCII-clean, self-scaffold regenerated (the `--instrument` dogfood path renders `instrument.md`), byte-identical default scaffold.

### `waiver-model`: the unified waiver record + baseline + W3/W5 (Q-44 pilot 2)

Complete (merged into main by fast-forward `7be5c2a`, 2026-07-18; RISKY, five rounds). The second `Q-44` pilot; ABSORBED `escalation-exempt` (`Q-40`) rather than building it as a third exemption special-case, and retired the `grandfathered`/`trivial` Roadmap statuses. Design record: the three `target-arch-{A,B,C}.md` (all three agree on the model). Scope: a `type:"waiver"` JSONL record carrying `unit` (`step` or `increment`), the `step` slug (+ optional `increment` token), `reason` (`predates-logging` | `review-skipped` | `accepted-at-escalation`), `evidence_tier` (`self-declared` | `record-backed`), and an `evidence` pointer REQUIRED iff `record-backed` (and forbidden otherwise); a single `type:"baseline"` marker record carrying the regime-start cutoff that both W3 and W4 consult for historical exemptions (replacing the 14 per-row `grandfathered` stamps and per-check historical boundaries); a new W5 check (every waiver names a real step; every `record-backed` waiver's `evidence` points at an existing matching `type:"escalation" human_decision:"decision"` record; the `reason`<->`evidence_tier` pairing is consistent so a self-declaration cannot claim the strong tier); and a W3 refactor to convergence-OR-waiver. Migrate THIS repo: the 14 `grandfathered` rows become `complete` + a `baseline` (or per-step `predates-logging` waivers), and `optional-modules` becomes `complete` + a `record-backed` `accepted-at-escalation` waiver for increment `optional-modules-inc2cii` pointing at its escalation record (`docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl`), which UNSTICKS the live-stuck step (SE-4). Load-bearing invariants to preserve (do NOT regress): the pause.md catch (a `complete` step with no records and no covering waiver still FAILS); exemptions stay declared+visible; the escalation waiver scope (fires only on `human_decision:"decision"`, one increment, streak-shortfall-only, risk_class-consistency and every other check still live); per-increment convergence (peak-over-increment streak); the two evidence tiers with no laundering (W5 enforces). `skipped` stays a distinct status (answers "is it done?" with "no"). Classify RISKY (it rewrites the enforcement backstop's exemption model) -> two consecutive clean rounds. After this, `Q-40` is resolved, `optional-modules` is closeable, and the exemption proliferation (SE-4, audit E2/E3/E4) is unified. The SE-2 metrics `Classification::Trivial` rename becomes trivial once the `trivial` status is gone (schedule it to follow). OUTCOME (2026-07-18): shipped the `type:"waiver"` record + `Waiver`/`Escalation` structs + `parse_waivers`/`parse_escalations`, the W5 integrity check, and the W3 convergence-OR-waiver refactor; retired `trivial`/`grandfathered` from `ROADMAP_STATUSES`. Two design choices were settled during the build (both refine the step spec above). (a) The 14 historical rows migrated as PER-UNIT `predates-logging`/`review-skipped` waivers, NOT a `baseline` step-cutoff: this matches the agreed `target-arch-A` incremental-path recommendation and avoids a positional/timestamp step boundary (the same moving-boundary fragility class the pilot-1 loop caught); the `baseline` record stays W4-only. 11 zero-record steps got `unit:step` `predates-logging` waivers; the 3 short-streak b2 steps (`convergence-accounting`, `pack-rebuild-tracking`, `user-prompts-dir`) got `unit:increment` `review-skipped` waivers (they have a lone `new_valid` round, so they did not predate logging); `optional-modules` got the `accepted-at-escalation` record-backed increment waiver for `optional-modules-inc2cii`, UNSTICKING it (now `complete`). (b) W5's record-backed join is UNIT-SCOPED (the cited escalation must be for the waived unit: `escalation.task == increment` for increment waivers, `leading_slug(task) == step` for step waivers) and increment waivers require `leading_slug(increment) == step`, closing two false-pass paths the round-1 review found (an unrelated/empty escalation, or a wrong-step waiver, earning the strong tier). The RISKY loop ran five rounds (R1 new_valid 9 [3 medium/6 low, separate triager]; R2 new_valid 2 low; R3 clean; R4 new_valid 1 low [F1, a test-coverage gap]; R5 clean), reaching peak `consecutive_clean` 1; the human (escalation, 2026-07-18) accepted convergence at streak 1 given two adversarial rounds found zero soundness/false-pass defects, after F1 was fixed. `waiver-model` itself is therefore exempted by its OWN `accepted-at-escalation` record-backed waiver (increment `waiver-model`) pointing at that escalation record, the mechanism dogfooding on the step that introduced it. Evidence: records 106-112 in `docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl` (5 rounds + the escalation + the self-waiver); `validate --workflow` holds with `waiver-model` and `optional-modules` and the 14 ex-`grandfathered` steps all `complete`. `Q-40` is resolved and `escalation-exempt` retired into this step.

### `structured-skeleton`: the clean-slate plan-as-data + `render` (Q-44 / Q-45, option B)

Next; the planning pass is DONE (2026-07-18) and this umbrella step is built in six reviewed increments defined at the end of this block. The `Q-45` skeleton-depth decision (human, 2026-07-18) chose B CLEAN-SLATE: the plan's human-authored SKELETON (Roadmap status + order + dependencies, the Open-Questions queue with id/status/ask/receipt-pointer and cross-references, the queue's `options`/`chosen` staying in the JSONL decision receipt rather than the TOML) moves into a structured `<task>.plan.toml`; the prose payloads (Step Details, the Status narrative, RESUME STATE) live in Markdown sidecars; and a new `agent-scaffold render` subcommand GENERATES a committed-but-never-hand-edited `<task>.md` from the two. This reaches Principle 8's fullest form (a structured single source of truth plus projection) and subsumes several audited sharp edges (B2/A1 the hand-synced Status line / RESUME STATE; B3 the triplicated status vocabulary; B6 the lexical round<->step link; edges (3) and (5)). Design record: `docs/plans/structured-architecture.explorations/target-arch-B-cleanslate.md` (the full clean-slate proposal, including a staged roadmap sketch). Accepted costs (from the decision): a render engine of several hundred lines, three-location editing (TOML skeleton, prose sidecar, generated view), a required render-before-commit step, `agent-scaffold`-on-PATH becoming mandatory for authoring, and a generated-file-must-not-be-hand-edited footgun that a dogfooded LLM-authored system must guard against (a golden render check + a do-not-edit banner). OWED FIRST: a planning pass (planner, using target-arch-B as the design input) to produce the concrete staged Roadmap increments, the Success-Criteria additions, the `<task>.plan.toml` schema, the render engine and its golden test, the migration of THIS repo's own plan and ledger (and how the existing JSONL `decision`/`waiver`/`baseline`/`escalation`/`round` records fit alongside the TOML skeleton), and the backwards-incompatibility handling, then plan review before implementation. This is a large, multi-increment initiative; classify per-increment at each loop open. DESIGN PASS DONE (2026-07-18): three independent explorers (`docs/plans/structured-skeleton.explorations/design-{A-minimal-schema,B-rich-schema,C-migration-safety}.md`) synthesised into `synthesis.md`. All three CONVERGE on the agreed core (a narrow `<task>.plan.toml` skeleton of `[meta]`/`[[step]]`/`[[question]]`; opaque Markdown prose sidecars spliced verbatim, no round-trip; a strict `render` engine `(toml, sidecars) -> <task>.md` with a do-not-edit banner + a `render --check` guard; enforcement preserved as a PARSER SWAP so W3/W4/W5 + the pause.md catch read `Step`/`Question` from a new `parse_toml`; typed `folded_into`/`blocked_by` cross-refs retiring the parametric status strings, SE-11, and the `blocked` status; a structured step<->increment id retiring the lexical `-inc<x>` strip, SE-10/B6; and a shadow-render-then-cutover migration keeping `validate` green at every stage) and confirm the enforcement substrate already shipped (pilots 1-2), so this is skeleton-relocation + render, not new enforcement. The ONE load-bearing split (waiver/baseline substrate) was DECIDED by the human as `Q-46`: MOVE them into the TOML (`[[step.waiver]]` + `[meta].w4_baseline`), so the target is the full design-B, the JSONL keeps only genuine events, W5's record-backed join becomes cross-substrate, and the historical JSONL waiver/baseline lines are pruned at cutover. Smaller sub-questions resolved on the synthesis recommendations (Principles structured `[[principle]]`, Success Criteria stay prose; queue `options`/`chosen` stay in the JSONL decision receipt (3(c)), where W4 reads them unchanged, and are NOT copied into the TOML; `render --check` warn-local/fail-CI; RESUME STATE stays ledger prose; declare the 5 orphan `task` slugs in `[meta]`; the ~1,200-word hand-authored Status narrative is preserved verbatim as a `_status-narrative.md` sidecar during migration and pruned later (3(f)); synthetic-pilot-first before the live plan). ROADMAP STRUCTURE (planner decision, 2026-07-18): kept as ONE umbrella Roadmap step (`structured-skeleton`) built in SIX reviewed increments tracked by `-inc<x>` task suffixes in the metrics log, each classified and converged per-increment, matching the `optional-modules` precedent rather than split into six sibling Roadmap slugs. Reasoning: the six increments are sub-parts of one migration (schema -> render -> enforcement-swap -> cutover -> scaffold), not independent features; one row keeps this decision history and the Q-45/Q-46 provenance in a single place; the Roadmap stays stable (every cross-reference to `structured-skeleton` survives); and the umbrella-plus-increments shape is exactly what W3 already handles for `optional-modules` (per-increment rounds join to the umbrella via the step<->increment link, and the pause.md catch still applies to the `complete` umbrella). The umbrella stays `next` until an increment starts (nothing is built yet). Note the mild recursion: Inc 2 retires the lexical `-inc<x>` strip, so this initiative's own later increments log under the new structured step/increment id it introduces, dogfooding the change.

Six increments (synthesis section 4, adapted for the Q-46 MOVE-waivers-and-baseline-to-TOML choice; risk class in parentheses, risky = touches the enforcement read-path or cuts over the live plan). Dependency order: Inc 1 -> Inc 3 -> Inc 4 -> Inc 5, with Inc 2 parallel to Inc 1/Inc 3 and Inc 6 after Inc 3 (sensibly after Inc 5, so the template reflects the final schema). Inc 1 and Inc 2 are the lowest-regret and go first. Classify each increment at loop open: risky increments need two consecutive clean rounds, low ones need one (the `decision-receipt`/`waiver-model` convention).

- Inc 1 `structured-skeleton-inc1` (low) BUILT (merged `27bd647`; LOW risk, converged over 2 rounds): the `<task>.plan.toml` schema (the full design-B rich schema per Q-46: `[meta]` with `title`/`w4_baseline`/`primary` (default `markdown`; when no `.plan.toml` is present, or `primary != "toml"`, the Markdown parser is the source, so the live repo stays Markdown-sourced until Inc 5's cutover)/the orphan-`task` list/the front-and-tail prose-sidecar references (the intro/motivations/repo-layout front matter and the Success-Criteria tail prose that render splices)/`render_sha256` (where the render hash lives: the digest `render --check` re-computes and compares, Inc 3); `[[step]]` with `slug`/`title`/`status`/`order`/`blocked_by`/`folds`, nested `[[step.increment]]` with `id`/`risk_class`, and nested `[[step.waiver]]` with `id`/`unit`/`increment`/`reason`/`evidence_tier`/`evidence`/`note`; `[[question]]` with `id`/`status`/`ask`/`folded_into` (when decided)/`superseded_by`/`receipt` (a pointer to the JSONL `type:"decision"` record by `q_id`; the queue's `options`/`chosen` live ONLY in that JSONL receipt, per 3(c), and are NOT carried in the TOML); `[[principle]]` with `n`/`name`/`text`), a strict `plan::parse_toml` returning a `PlanToml` struct (a superset of the specced `(Vec<Step>, Vec<Question>, Meta)` tuple: it also carries the parsed `[[principle]]` array; serde `deny_unknown_fields` is set on every plan.toml struct per the human's O4=STRICT decision, so a typo'd key is a loud parse error, while the JSONL/metrics log keeps its forward-compatible stance), and `validate --source` (schema types/enums + internal cross-references: unique slugs, unique increment ids, `blocked_by`/`folds`/`folded_into`/`superseded_by` resolve, `decided` implies a resolving `folded_into`, well-formed ids, in-set statuses, the `[meta]` orphan list well-formed, and the WAIVER field-presence + `reason`<->`evidence_tier` pairing rules moved verbatim from `check_record`). No render; nothing reads the TOML in anger; the live plan stays Markdown-sourced. DEPENDS ON: nothing. Risk: LOW (a pure addition, unconsulted by W3/W4/W5). ACCEPTANCE: `parse_toml` round-trips a fixture skeleton including nested waivers; `validate --source` flags an injected bad status, a dangling `blocked_by`, a `decided` question with no `folded_into`, and a malformed waiver (a `record-backed` waiver missing `evidence`, a `self-declared` waiver dressed as `record-backed`, and an `increment` waiver whose named increment is absent); `validate --plan` on the live repo stays green.
- Inc 2 `structured-skeleton-inc2` (low, parallel) BUILT (merged `fd1144d`; LOW risk, converged over 2 rounds): the structured step/increment link on JSONL records. Add an optional structured step (and increment) id to new `round` and `escalation` records; W3/W5 prefer it and fall back to `leading_slug` for pre-migration records. (Waivers do NOT gain the field, since Q-46 moves them to the TOML; only genuine events keep a JSONL home.) Additive; retires the SE-10/B6 over-strip risk (T3) for new data. DEPENDS ON: nothing (parallel to Inc 1/Inc 3). Risk: LOW (an additive JSONL field, off the enforcement-swap path). ACCEPTANCE: a `round`/`escalation` record carrying the field joins without the lexical strip; a pre-migration record with no field still joins via the `leading_slug` shim; the existing W3/W5 suite passes.
- Inc 3 `structured-skeleton-inc3` (risky) BUILT (merged `1e1d61b`; RISKY, converged over 5 rounds, 2 consecutive clean): the `render` engine + the `render --check` guard. A strict pure `(plan.toml, sidecars) -> <task>.md`: opaque sidecar splicing (no round-trip), the derived Status line (step-status distribution + open-question count + waiver counts read from the TOML), the generated status-vocabulary fragment (from the code constants, closing B3), the do-not-edit banner naming the real sources, the inline waiver Notes in the rendered Roadmap (waivers now live in the TOML), strict failure that writes NOTHING on a broken source, and the check (byte/golden or the `[meta].render_sha256` hash; warn-local, fail-CI per synthesis section 3(d)). Tested against a fixture `.plan.toml` + sidecars and the synthetic pilot plan (3f). DEPENDS ON: Inc 1 (schema/parser). Risk: RISKY (a new load-bearing derived-artifact write path). ACCEPTANCE: render is deterministic and byte-stable; `render --check` fails on a one-byte hand-edit of the generated `.md` and on a stale render after a source edit; render exits non-zero and writes nothing on a missing sidecar or an unresolved cross-reference.
- Inc 4 `structured-skeleton-inc4` (risky) BUILT (merged `8017a2c`; RISKY, converged over 3 rounds, 2 consecutive clean): point the checks at the TOML source, NOW INCLUDING waivers + baseline (Q-46). W3/W4/W5 and `status` read steps/questions/waivers from `parse_toml` and the baseline from `[meta].w4_baseline` when `[meta].primary == "toml"` (else the Markdown + JSONL-waiver fallback, so the live repo is unaffected until Inc 5). W3 joins rounds to the declared `[[step.increment]].id` (by Inc 2's structured increment id when the round record carries it, else the `leading_slug` strip, so Inc 4 uses whatever join exists and does not hard-depend on Inc 2) and reads `[[step.waiver]]`; W4's decided-gate becomes `status == "decided"` reading `folded_into`, and W4 reads its cutoff from `[meta].w4_baseline`; W5 reads `[[step.waiver]]` from the TOML and joins each record-backed waiver's `evidence` to the JSONL `type:"escalation" human_decision:"decision"` record. The join is now CROSS-SUBSTRATE (a mutable TOML declaration joined to immutable JSONL evidence); the evidence is still the immutable escalation, so the property survives, but the join spans two files with different mutation rules. The two evidence tiers stay un-launderable and the pause.md catch is unchanged. Tested against TOML fixtures. DEPENDS ON: Inc 1. Risk: RISKY (rewrites the enforcement checks' plan-side source AND the waiver/baseline source, and introduces the cross-substrate W5 join; a larger risky surface than the synthesis's keep-in-JSONL variant, per the Q-46 decision). ACCEPTANCE: the full `workflow.rs` suite passes with `primary = "toml"` fixtures; the pause.md catch and BOTH cross-substrate accepted-at-escalation record-backed waivers behave identically reading the TOML waiver joined to the JSONL escalation: the `optional-modules-inc2cii` waiver (joined to its escalation record) and the `waiver-model` self-waiver on increment `waiver-model` (joined to the `waiver-model` escalation, the self-referential dogfooding case); with `primary = "markdown"` the live repo is unaffected.
- Inc 5 `structured-skeleton-inc5` (risky; reversible cutover) BUILT (merged in the Inc 5 cutover; RISKY, converged over 2 clean rounds): migrate THIS repo, synthetic-pilot-first (3f). Generate the skeleton (scripted from the existing parsers), split the prose into sidecars (the heavy manual lift, ~51 step bodies + ~46 question bodies + the meta prose, including the ~1,200-word hand-authored Status narrative preserved verbatim as a `_status-narrative.md` sidecar during migration and pruned later, per synthesis 3(f); the single place a data-loss slip would hide), TRANSLATE the 16 historical JSONL `waiver` records into `[[step.waiver]]` entries and the 1 `baseline` record into `[meta].w4_baseline` (the Q-46 re-home), keep `[meta].primary = "markdown"` and shadow-render + fidelity-diff against the current `.md` (expected diffs: banner, derived Status line, question-body relocation, inline waiver Notes; and the `_status-narrative.md` editorial narrative, distinct from the derived Status line, spliced back verbatim and expected to round-trip with no diff), then CUT OVER in one atomic commit: set `primary = "toml"`, render, PRUNE the 16 `waiver` + 1 `baseline` lines from the JSONL (the deliberate one-time append-only break Q-46 sanctions), and commit the TOML + sidecars + generated `.md` + pruned JSONL together; wire `render --check` into `.agents/checks.toml` + CI. DEPENDS ON: Inc 3 (shadow render) and Inc 4 (TOML-sourced checks at cutover). Risk: its W3 `risk_class` is `risky` (its round records are logged `risky` and its convergence bar is two consecutive clean rounds); "reversible" is a descriptive blast-radius property, not a third `risk_class` value. It cuts over the live plan and breaks append-only once, but every pre-cutover stage is additive and reversible, and the cutover is a single `git revert`-able commit that restores both the hand-authored `.md` and the pruned JSONL lines. ACCEPTANCE: on the synthetic pilot and then the live repo, the generated `.md` matches the reviewed plan modulo the documented expected diffs; after cutover `validate --source`, `validate --workflow`, and `status` are green reading the TOML with waivers/baseline sourced from it, and the JSONL retains only genuine events; both record-backed waivers (`optional-modules-inc2cii` and the `waiver-model` self-waiver) are translated into `[[step.waiver]]` entries with their `evidence` re-pointed to the retained JSONL escalations, and W5 passes for both across the substrate split; `git revert` of the single cutover commit restores the hand-authored `.md` and the pruned JSONL lines to green.
- Inc 6 `structured-skeleton-inc6` (risky) BUILT (merged `ce0d36c`; RISKY, converged at clean-streak 1 by the human fix-and-converge-now decision on a trivial orthogonal finding, exempted by an `accepted-at-escalation` waiver whose escalation records that proactive convergence call): the pack template + scaffold, plus the pack's doc/prompt currency work (Q-47). Replace `pack/plan-template.md` with `pack/plan-template.plan.toml` (a minimal skeleton: a `[meta]` `title` placeholder, no `w4_baseline` and `primary = "toml"`, one seeded `[[principle]]`, one `[[step]]` placeholder, an empty queue, the `[[step.waiver]]` schema available-but-unused) + starter sidecar directories (`.steps/.gitkeep`, `.questions/.gitkeep`) + the front/tail prose sidecars; `scaffold` drops the TOML template and runs an initial `render`. Update the pack manifest wiring in `pack/pack.toml` so the template `[[asset]]` maps the new `plan-template.plan.toml` (and its sidecar drop) rather than `plan-template.md` -> `docs/plans/TEMPLATE.md`. Migrate or drop `plan_template_documents_every_accepted_status` (render now generates the vocabulary from the constants, so the live-plan drift guard, B3, is subsumed and the guard shrinks to the template). DOC/PROMPT CURRENCY (Q-47, the human decided all doc/prompt currency lands inside Inc 6, choosing the single-coherent-pack option over splitting the coherence-critical prompts into Inc 6 with a separate docs-sync step for the rest, and over a separate step after Inc 6; accepted cost: a larger Inc 6 diff for a pack that is never internally inconsistent): bring the shipped pack's prompts and docs current with the TOML/render/sidecar flow so a freshly scaffolded project describes the plan flow the tool actually implements. Concretely: (1) `pack/prompts/orchestrator.md` and `pack/prompts/planner.md` (and any other `pack/prompts/*.md` referencing the plan format or `TEMPLATE.md`) move from "author a Markdown plan from `docs/plans/TEMPLATE.md`" to the TOML+render+sidecar authoring AND editing flow (edit the `.plan.toml` skeleton and the prose sidecars, run `agent-scaffold render`, never hand-edit the generated `<task>.md`, and `render --check` before committing both the source and the generated view together); (2) `pack/user-prompts/kickoff.md` ("start one from `docs/plans/TEMPLATE.md`") is updated to the TOML template + render start; (3) `README.md` describes the render/TOML/sidecar projection architecture where it currently implies a Markdown plan (its layout listing of `docs/plans/TEMPLATE.md`, its workflow prose, and its command reference, which today shows only `validate --plan`/`status --plan` and mentions neither `render`/`render --check` nor `validate --source`); (4) `pack/AGENTS.md` is reconciled to the render architecture: its phase-2 wording ("draft a plan under `docs/plans/` from `docs/plans/TEMPLATE.md`") is updated DIRECTLY to the TOML/render/sidecar flow (edit the `.plan.toml` skeleton plus prose sidecars, run `agent-scaffold render`, never hand-edit the generated `<task>.md`, `render --check` before committing both), the load-bearing altitude sub-question now DECIDED as `Q-48` (describe the TOML/render flow directly, chosen over keeping it abstract), so this phase-2 edit is no longer gated; and by the SAME Q-48 decision the line-57 Tracking-progress status-SSOT wording ("the status table ... the single source of truth for step status; the implementer keeps it current") is reconciled to name `[[step]].status` in the `.plan.toml` as the status source of truth and the generated Roadmap table as a projection the implementer never hand-edits; (5) DROPPED as already-covered: the earlier premise (that `pack/instrument.md` does not document the `type:"decision"` record's `task` requirement) was wrong; `pack/instrument.md:3`'s preamble already states every metrics record carries the required `task` field, the decision receipt included, so nothing is owed here. Whether to restate `task` per-type on the `decision` bullet for self-containment is a Principle-20 choice deferred to the separate doc-redundancy decision, NOT scoped into Inc 6. (6) STATUS-MAINTENANCE currency: the shipped prompts still tell a scaffolded implementer that the Roadmap TABLE is the status source of truth to hand-edit, which is stale under the TOML-primary flow this increment ships (`primary = "toml"`, where the status source of truth is `[[step]].status` in the `.plan.toml` and the generated Roadmap table is a `render`-overwritten, `render --check`-guarded projection). `pack/prompts/implementer.md:5` ("set the step's Status cell in the Roadmap table, which is the single source of truth for status") is corrected to: the source of truth for status is `[[step]].status` in the `.plan.toml`; edit it there and re-render, and never hand-edit the generated Roadmap table in `<task>.md`. `pack/prompts/orchestrator.md:31` ("the Roadmap is the single source of truth for step status") is reconciled the same way (the TOML `[[step]].status` is the source; the Roadmap is a projection). The matching `pack/AGENTS.md:57` Tracking-progress status-SSOT claim is the SAME class as the Q-48-decided phase-2 sentence and is handled in item (4) under that decision, reconciled directly to the TOML/render flow. DEPENDS ON: Inc 3 (render) and Inc 4 (the TOML-source `validate` path, since the template sets `primary = "toml"` and needs `validate` to pass on a TOML-sourced project with no Markdown plan); sensibly follows Inc 5 (the final schema). Risk: RISKY (it changes what every scaffolded project inherits, now including its prompts and docs). ACCEPTANCE: a fresh `agent-scaffold` run drops a coherent `plan.toml` + rendered `<task>.md` + starter sidecar directories, and `validate` passes on it; a fresh project has no `w4_baseline`, so every decision it makes correctly requires a receipt; a fresh scaffold's prompts describe the TOML/render/sidecar authoring AND editing flow, with no stale `TEMPLATE.md` or Markdown-only-plan authoring references left in `pack/prompts/` or `pack/user-prompts/kickoff.md`; `README.md` and `pack/AGENTS.md` describe the render/TOML/sidecar architecture (README's command reference covers `render`/`render --check` and `validate --source`), with `pack/AGENTS.md`'s phase-2 wording and its line-57 Tracking-progress wording describing the TOML/render flow directly per the decided `Q-48`; the shipped prompts and `pack/AGENTS.md` do not instruct hand-editing the generated Roadmap status table, and a fresh scaffold's implementer is directed to maintain status by editing `[[step]].status` in the `.plan.toml` and re-rendering; `just scaffold-self` regenerates the root `.agents/` + root `AGENTS.md` + root `README.md` clean so deployed == pack; and `render --check` and `validate` (source + workflow) stay green.

### `state-queries`: superseded by `workflow-driver` Stage 1, which built `next` + `status --resume` (`Q-28`; `skipped`)

Skipped. Superseded by `workflow-driver` Stage 1 (`Q-51`), which delivered exactly this step's scope: the advisory `agent-scaffold next` projection (the forward member of the workflow projection family) plus the `status --resume` slice that echoes the ledger's `## RESUME STATE` verbatim, both reusing the W3 streak/cap arithmetic so the forward projection agrees with `validate --workflow`'s backward check. The `state-queries` step schema has no `superseded` status (that is a question-only status), so it is retired as `skipped` with the supersession named here, following the `ledger-parse` precedent. The original decided scope is preserved below for the record. Decided (`Q-28`, human); re-scoped by `round-log-core` (`Q-34`). Two thin projections over a derived `WorkflowState` assembled from `src/plan.rs` (Roadmap status + Open Questions) + `src/metrics.rs` (the JSONL round log, when present) + a verbatim `## RESUME STATE` section extract (the ~10-line extractor absorbed from the superseded `ledger-parse`). `agent-scaffold next` emits the single authoritative next required action (derived from the same transition logic `workflow-invariants` checks, so `next` computes the legal next step and the enforcement check verifies the recorded state did not skip one). Realise `state` as an enhanced `status` rather than a third verb: `status` gains a compact default (current step and status from the plan, open-status questions, current streak from the round log when instrumented) with the full `{plan, metrics}` projection behind `--json`, plus a `--resume` mode that also echoes the ledger's `## RESUME STATE` section verbatim (human-authored narrative, extractable not derivable). This is the mechanism that REMOVES the incentive to hand-maintain a duplicate status in RESUME STATE (the `round-log-core` de-dup discipline): the generated view is authoritative for "where are we", so RESUME STATE stays a pointer plus transient state. Token-efficiency reality: the win is real only at cold resume after a compaction and in a CI/hook, and negative per-turn during active work, so scope to the resume and check paths, repoint `resume.md`/`compaction-prep.md` at `next`/`status --resume`, and do not poll each turn. Explicitly NOT built: a separate `state` verb or a wholesale navigator. Depends on `round-log-core`; reuses `workflow-invariants`' transition logic; no `src/ledger.rs`.

### `reviewer-harness-field`: Record the reviewer's harness in the round-log attribution (`Q-37`)

Not started. Decided (`Q-37`, human, after the `reviewer-diversity` interim metrics read, 2026-07-17). Add an OPTIONAL `harness` string to each element of the `round` record's `reviewers[]` attribution array in `docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl`, so the calibration log records WHICH harness (CLI) a reviewer ran on, not just its model. Motivation: `reviewer-diversity` generalised the diversity rule to "models OR harnesses", but the metrics schema's per-reviewer object carries only `{role, model, raw_findings, valid_findings}`, so a future cross-harness proof-of-concept could not be measured; the interim read of the 66-record log surfaced this gap. Scope: a one-field, non-breaking addition, validated as a string WHEN PRESENT (the 20 existing attributed records omit it and must still validate; the `reviewers[]` array stays optional and its four existing per-reviewer fields stay required; unknown extra fields are already permitted, so the point is to make `harness` a first-class, validated, documented field rather than an ignored unknown). Touches `src/metrics.rs` (`require_reviewers` gains the optional `harness` string check plus its doc comment), `pack/instrument.md` (document the optional `harness` field in the `reviewers` array description, so the code-vs-prose drift guard covers it), the drift-guard test (assert `harness` appears backtick-wrapped in the prose), and validator tests (a present string `harness` accepted; a non-string `harness` reported). LOW-risk (additive, off the scaffold path, tested). NOT in scope, deferred to `workflow-calibration`: a unique/marginal-valid count that would let the log answer "how much of the second reviewer's yield was unique" (needs a design pass on per-finding identity versus a derived count, so it is not bundled into this mechanical field addition).

### `reviewer-diversity`: Generalise the reviewer/triager diversity rule to models OR harnesses (`Q-36` part a)

Not started. Decided (`Q-36`, human, after the two-explorer cross-harness design pass; design record in `docs/plans/cross-harness-isolation.explorations/q33-q36-{A,B}.md`). A SMALL, always-on core-docs edit: generalise the existing reviewer/triager model-diversity rule from "prefer several reviewers with different lenses, and different models where available" to "different models OR HARNESSES where available", so a user who runs multiple CLIs (for example Claude Code plus another model's CLI) is guided to spread reviewers across harnesses too, minimising the shared blind spots that same-model (and same-harness) reviewers share. Harness-agnostic, so it ships as core content, not a module: a one-clause edit to `pack/AGENTS.md` (the Reviewers role line and the Design-explorations "different lenses or models" line) and to the `pack/user-prompts/review.md` depth-and-lenses cue, then regenerate the self-scaffold. (`pack/prompts/reviewer.md` and `pack/prompts/triager.md`, named as candidate targets during planning, carry no diversity clause to generalise, so the build deliberately left them unchanged: reviewer/triager diversity is an orchestrator-spawning concern, canonical in `pack/AGENTS.md`, one source of truth. The `review.md` cue was the one further user-facing surface the review loop found still framing diversity as models-only, and it was generalised too.) This is the ADOPT-NOW half of the `Q-33` / `Q-36` cross-harness decision; the DEFER half (the `.agents/spawn.toml` launch-map and the container-wrapper shim that would let the orchestrator actually spawn cross-harness agents) is recorded as the deferred growth path in `optional-modules` increment (3). Judgment call (planner): kept as its own small step rather than folded into an existing one, so the Roadmap and the `validate --plan` cross-reference each carry a slug for the always-on rule; it has no code and no dependencies, so it can land at any time.

### `review-mode`: Human-invokable review as a fourth entry mode (`Q-35`)

Not started. Decided (`Q-35`, human, after a two-independent-explorer design pass; design record in `docs/plans/review-mode.explorations/review-entry-mode-{A,B}.md`). Add a fourth ENTRY MODE to the one workflow: the acceptance pass promoted to a human-invokable entry, so a human can prompt the agent to review a codebase (or a diff) against a plan and/or specific constraints and get a findings report. The tool is converging on ONE workflow with several entry modes (`implement` via `kickoff.md`, `explore` via `explore.md`, `review` via a new `review.md`), each a different entry and terminal over the same roles and durable artifacts; `review` reuses the review machinery wholesale (independent reviewers with distinct lenses, a separate triager, findings files, the four-level severity scale plus the high/critical dismissal-recheck backstop), so it adds no new phase or role and staffs only the read-only roles (no implement phase).

Decided shape:

- Entry: a thin `pack/user-prompts/review.md` mirroring `kickoff.md` / `explore.md`, registered in `pack.toml` and covered by the manifest asset-list test.
- Terminal: a SINGLE-PASS reviewers-then-triager (the acceptance variant, not the consecutive-clean convergence loop), KEEPING the high/critical dismissal recheck, ending in a COMMITTED, KEPT report (`docs/plans/<task>.review-report.md` or `.reviews/report.md`; the planner picks the exact path at build time) with a paste-into-`kickoff` handoff so the human can turn valid findings into an implement task. A minimal one-round ledger is created for the run and deleted at close (commit-before-delete).
- TARGET: a diff range or the whole tree at HEAD, reusing the reviewer before/after-or-range vocabulary.
- CRITERIA (composable): a named plan's Success Criteria, plus ad-hoc constraints/conditions, plus an always-present principles floor (open-ended correctness/quality).
- Instrumentation: under `--instrument`, the run logs one `round` record with a NEW `review` phase value; add `Review => "review"` to the `Phase` enum in `src/metrics.rs`, document it in `pack/instrument.md`, and extend the validator tests. This is the only code beyond prompt/pack content.
- A one-clause generalization of `pack/prompts/reviewer.md` and `pack/prompts/triager.md` so "the plan" degrades to "the criteria you were given" when a review run has no plan.

This SUBSUMES the whole-codebase acceptance-review LATER job noted elsewhere in this plan; that job becomes an instance of this mode. Footprint: mostly pack content plus the one `Phase` enum value plus the `review.md` pack.toml/manifest registration.

### `test-driven`: Test-driven process module (`Q-30`)

Not started; after `optional-modules` increment 2 (dependency-forced: it composes with the checks module). Decided (`Q-30`, human, after a two-independent-explorer design pass; design record in `docs/plans/test-modules.explorations/test-driven-and-mutation-{A,B}.md`). An OPTIONAL module that expands the planner's role with a per-step test spec (cases, inputs/expected outputs, properties, end-to-end flows) and inserts a test-authoring phase before implementation (tests written to fail first, reviewed and triaged, then code implemented until they pass), so an implementation is deterministically checked against the plan, the same "verify, don't trust the LLM" ethos as the metrics validator and the checks module.

Decided design:

- Dependency: the module HARD-DEPENDS on the checks module and auto-enables it via the `requires` field on `[[module]]` (the field the `{{modules}}` slot carries, per `optional-modules` increment 2).
- Roles: a SEPARATE `test-author` writer role (distinct from the implementer), so the test stays an independent spec of the plan's intent rather than a self-graded artifact (role separation, no agent grades its own work).
- Execution single-sourced: test execution is a `kind = "test"` entry in the checks-owned `.agents/checks.toml` (a run command plus a compile-only `check` command), so it composes with the deterministic-checks module rather than duplicating a runner.
- Two artifacts, two gate profiles (the phase-4 split): the TEST artifact's red profile = compiles + fails by assertion + covers the plan's test spec + non-vacuous; the CODE artifact's green profile = compiles + lints clean + all pre-authored tests pass. Red/green is a PHASE PARAMETER handed to the checks-reviewer, NOT a `checks.toml` field (the config stays declarative; the phase says which profile applies). Interface stubs are folded into the test artifact so the tests compile before the code exists. A frozen-tests git-diff tripwire flags any change to the authored tests during the green phase (so the code is made to pass the tests, not the tests weakened to pass the code).
- Risk-scaling: opt-in and matched to risk class, reusing the existing convergence risk classification (collapse the ceremony for low-risk steps), since the two-artifact split roughly doubles the reviewed-artifact count. Two new Roadmap steps (`test-driven` then `mutation`) land AFTER `optional-modules` increment 2 (dependency-forced order). Related: `mutation` (`Q-31`).

### `mutation`: Mutation-testing module (`Q-31`)

Not started; after `test-driven` (dependency-forced). Decided (`Q-31`, human, same design pass; design record in `docs/plans/test-modules.explorations/test-driven-and-mutation-{A,B}.md`). An OPTIONAL module layered ON TOP of `test-driven` that mutates the code and confirms the tests kill the mutants, verifying the tests are robust rather than vacuous (adversarial verification applied to the tests themselves). Conceptual hierarchy, each layer verifying the one below: checks module (runs lint/format/test) -> `test-driven` -> `mutation`.

Decided design:

- Dependency: HARD-DEPENDS on `test-driven` (auto-enabled via `requires`), which in turn requires checks.
- Config: a `kind = "mutation"` entry in `.agents/checks.toml` (with the optional `budget` / `threshold` fields the checks schema reserves).
- Cadence: fires ONCE per step AFTER green convergence (not every round), diff-scoped (mutates only the step's changed code), time-budgeted, and risk-scaled (risky steps only), reusing the existing convergence risk classification so cost is spent where suite quality matters.
- Review: reuses the checks-reviewer at a DISTINCT gate (no separate mutation-reviewer role); a SURVIVING mutant is a finding that routes back to the TEST artifact (the tests, not the code, are strengthened).
- Graceful degradation: when no mutation tool exists for the language, the module degrades to a RECORDED skip rather than a failure (tooling is language-specific and less universal than test runners, for example `cargo-mutants`, `mutmut`, Stryker).

### `workflow-driver`: the staged workflow DRIVER, the forward member of a projection family over the workflow (`Q-51`)

Not started; the build-start is a separate pending human decision, so this umbrella step stays `not-started` with no increments declared yet (the per-stage `[[step.increment]]` declarations land at each stage's close-out, mirroring `optional-modules` and `structured-skeleton`). Design shape DECIDED (`Q-51`, human, 2026-07-19): the human chose the FULL DRIVER as the target (over the four round-1 explorers' advisory-MVP-only recommendation), staged so no increment hardens a still-evolving workflow. Design record: `docs/plans/mealy-workflow-driver.explorations/r2-synthesis.md` (the authoritative full-driver design + staged build plan), backed by the four `r2-*.md` lens files (`r2-requirements-scope.md`, `r2-user-flows.md`, `r2-design-space-vision.md`, `r2-architecture-build-path.md`) and the round-1 `synthesis.md`. This is a MAJOR initiative, larger than `structured-skeleton`; classify each stage's risk at its own loop open (the risk class in parentheses below is the design-pass estimate).

North star (the projection-family framing). The driver is the FORWARD member of a family of folds over two durable sources: the append-only event log `docs/metrics/workflow.jsonl` and a data-driven control spec `.agents/workflow.toml`. The family: `validate --workflow` is the BACKWARD check (did the past obey the rules), `status` is the SUMMARY, `next` is the FORWARD execution (given the state, what the rules dictate next), and `render` extends the family to prose (it generates the AGENTS.md control fragments from the same spec, so process logic and process prose cannot drift). So the driver is not a new subsystem bolted on; it is the missing forward member of a family whose backward and summary members already ship, and it reuses the W3/W4/W5 reconstruction wholesale (the convergence arithmetic `src/workflow.rs` already runs to CHECK a completed history, run forward to EMIT the next move).

The data-driven-constants spec plus the Rust transition function. `.agents/workflow.toml` is a tool-owned, pack-shipped reference asset (the same for every scaffolded project; `agent-scaffold`'s own repo dogfoods its scaffolded copy) that holds ONLY the deterministic CONTROL CONSTANTS and orderings: the risk-class -> required-clean-streak map, the total-round cap, the backstop severity threshold, the phase/loop sequence, the isolation-tier preference order, the role -> prompt-path map, and the findings/ledger/exploration/report path templates. The transition FUNCTION stays Rust (the streak/cap arithmetic the W3 checker already computes, run forward). The split is deliberate: a workflow VALUE change (a constant, a threshold, an added ordering) is a data edit reviewable like a plan edit; only a genuinely new rule KIND is a code change to the interpreter. This is the answer to "keep it data-driven so the workflow evolves without pouring concrete": the parts that change often are data, the fixed interpreter is small code. Both `validate --workflow` and the driver parse one `WorkflowSpec`, which single-sources today's hardcoded `RiskClass::required_streak` (currently duplicated against AGENTS.md prose). A built-in default (`WorkflowSpec::builtin()` equal to today's constants) covers a project shipping no spec, pinned by a `builtin()`-equals-old-constants assertion so an un-migrated project validates byte-for-byte unchanged.

The control-vs-judgment boundary, enforced by types. The tool owns CONTROL (counting streaks, sequencing phases, scheduling the ready frontier, gating the backstop, resolving the isolation tier, emitting prompts); it CONSUMES agent/human JUDGMENT as typed `Input` variants (a triage verdict `{clean | new_valid}`, a recheck result, a human decision, a commit, an override) and NEVER manufactures a verdict. The typed engine (Stage 2) makes this load-bearing: `step` has no branch that produces an `outcome`, a `risk_class`, or a `human_decision`, so the type system enforces the boundary. Getting this wrong in the permissive direction hallucinates decisions; in the restrictive direction it straitjackets the agent. This is the primary correctness property.

Terminal authority (ORQ-1, decided) and the other open-requirements resolutions. The driver's END STATE is ADVISORY + validated event-RECORDING, NON-BLOCKING in-loop, with an OPT-IN boundary gate (`validate --workflow --strict` in CI or a pre-commit hook, reusing the existing checks/hook pattern) for hard enforcement at the LANDING boundary. So the loop never deadlocks if the tool is skipped, but the boundary gate catches an unrecorded gap before work lands; this also answers ORQ-2 (gate at the boundary, not per-step, keeping the loop robust, harness-agnostic, least-authority, and respecting Q-24's no-runtime-write-path-dependency stance). ORQ-3 (multi-repo) defaults to single-project (a far-stage YAGNI). ORQ-4 (spec-change ceremony): `workflow.toml` is load-bearing config, so edits go through the normal plan + review like AGENTS.md changes, no new ceremony. ORQ-5 (reminder cadence): light, each `next` surfaces only the reminders relevant to the current action, not a per-call dump that trains agents to ignore it.

Complements, does not subsume. The driver COMPLEMENTS (does not replace) the re-grounding + provenance step and the `Q-50` doc-currency guidance: they address different drift axes (the driver fights CONTROL-drift, an agent forgetting a rule that applies right here; re-grounding fights CONTEXT-drift; Q-50 fights DOC-TRUTH-drift). Q-50 is in fact a PREREQUISITE if the driver emits prompts assembled from the role-prompt files (a driver is only as correct as those files). Those cheaper items un-gate and ship on their own schedule rather than waiting on this.

Staged increments (each a reviewable increment; classify risk at loop open). The committed near-term path is 0a -> 0b -> 1 -> 2, which realises the driver's core (the data-driven spec, the advisory surface, the typed engine) while keeping the workflow evolvable as data; Stage 3+ stays behind evidence gates.

- Stage 0a (RISKY, no deps): the `.agents/workflow.toml` control-constants spec + `WorkflowSpec` + `WorkflowSpec::builtin()`; single-source the convergence constants into the W3 check (replace `RiskClass::required_streak`'s hardcoded `match` with a spec lookup, thread the spec into `w3_problems`, add the round cap as `spec.round_cap()`); add a `--workflow-spec` flag on `validate`. Behaviour-preserving, guarded by the existing W3 tests plus the `builtin()`-equals-old-constants assertion. RISKY because it touches the safety-relevant convergence bar and adds a scaffolded asset every downstream project inherits, but it changes no behaviour. VALUE independent of the driver: closes the live `required_streak`/AGENTS.md duplication (a real bug-closing win) and lays the data-driven-spec foundation the whole driver rests on. Deps: none (builds on the shipped `structured-skeleton` + `validate --workflow`). ACCEPTANCE: `validate --workflow` on the live repo is byte-for-byte unchanged with the built-in default; the `builtin()`-equals-old-constants assertion holds; a spec with an altered `required_streak` changes the enforced bar the checker applies (`round_cap` and the backstop severity ship as spec data in Stage 0a but are not W3-enforced until a later stage, so they do not alter the bar yet).
- Stage 0b (RISKY, deps 0a): generate the AGENTS.md control-constant fragment from the spec via a pack `{{workflow_control}}` render slot (the streak-per-class sentence, the cap, the backstop severity, the tier order, the path conventions), byte-compare guarded exactly as the plan's generated status vocabulary already is. This is the PACK generation path (`build_assets` filling the guidance template's slots at scaffold time), not `plan::render`; the hand-authored rationale prose stays around the slot. Proves the code-vs-prose generation closure on the smallest, highest-value content before it is widened. Deps: 0a (the spec). ACCEPTANCE: the generated fragment matches the spec constants; the byte-compare check fails on a hand edit of the generated fragment and on a stale fragment after a spec edit.
- Stage 1 (LOW_RISK, deps 0a): the advisory, read-only, stateless `agent-scaffold next` MVP, largely the already-planned `state-queries` step (`Q-28`/`Q-34`) plus a next-action reminder. For the single active review loop it forward-projects the W3 reconstruction to emit the current per-unit state, the valid transitions, and the next action as a filled instruction prompt (the role prompt path from the spec, the artifact and diff range from the ledger's `## RESUME STATE`, the findings/ledger paths from the spec templates, and the phase-keyed Principle reminders), in both JSON and human text. No typed fleet, no scheduler, no write-path. Stateless: recompute from the durable files each call (no `.fsm-state` store), so it survives a crash or compaction. LOW_RISK (read-only, reuses the shipped parsers). Deps: 0a (reads the spec); coordinate with `state-queries` to avoid duplicating the `status --resume` slice. VALUE: the point-of-action anti-drift reminder that captures most of the drift-reduction value, and the EVIDENCE SOURCE (adherence rate, drift reduction over ~10 step cycles) that gates every later stage. When the next action is an awaiting-human-decision gate, the reminder set includes the human-input-contract reminder (present the options, their trade-offs, a recommendation, and Principle-judged reasoning), folded here from `human-input-gate-reinforce` part (b) (`Q-54`): this is the point-of-action reinforcement for a rule the orchestrator was observed drifting off, so it is the driver's first concrete anti-drift reminder and its own live evidence. ACCEPTANCE: `next` emits the correct next action and filled prompt for a fixture plan + log + ledger; its forward projection of the streak/cap matches the W3 checker's backward computation on the same files; the awaiting-human-decision reminder carries the human-input-contract cue; output is deterministic across machines.
- Stage 2 (RISKY, deps 1): the typed `src/driver/` FSM engine. First EXTRACT the streak arithmetic out of `round_log_consistency_problems`/`w3_problems` into a shared `reconstruct_loop(rounds, spec) -> LoopState` that BOTH the checker and the driver call (one arithmetic, two directions, made literal code reuse), so the driver and W3 provably cannot diverge. Then add `driver/fsm.rs` (a spec-parameterized `ReviewLoop` Mealy machine with a TOTAL `step` transition function, plus nested `StepMachine`/`TaskMachine`), `driver/reconstruct.rs` (the full per-unit fleet from `PlanToml` + the round log + the ledger + the spec), and `driver/emit.rs` (filled instruction prompts from the spec's role/path templates). The nesting makes "a `complete` step whose review loop never converged" unrepresentable by construction (the same invariant W3 checks post-hoc), and the total `step` makes an undefined transition unrepresentable. RISKY (the core logic; must match the checker exactly). Deps: Stage 1 (the advisory surface) and Stage 0a (the spec). ACCEPTANCE: exhaustive transition-table tests (every `(state, input)` pair has a defined image); a DIFFERENTIAL test replaying a round log forward through `step` yields the same streak `round_log_consistency_problems` computes; golden fixtures for the `next` output; every `Output` is reachable only from an `Input` carrying the corresponding judgment (the control-vs-judgment boundary tested).
- Stage 3+ (GATED on evidence): each stage stays behind its own evidence gate so no increment hardens a still-molten workflow. The `blocked_by` ready-frontier scheduler (`driver/schedule.rs`, a pure step-granularity graph function proposing the antichain of ready steps the orchestrator may fan out; GATE: real parallel multi-unit execution actually occurring, since today steps run one at a time). Generation-widening (extend 0b from the constants to the sequencing/paths/tiers fragments; GATE: 0b's constant fragment proven correct and readable). This stage also owes a pluralize-by-count fix to `control_fragment()`, which hardcodes the singular/plural nouns ("clean round", "is 5 rounds") that are grammatical only for the shipped 1/2/5 values; a future data edit (for example `cap = 1`) would render ungrammatical prose (deferred here from the Stage 0b review as finding L-1, non-blocking because 0b only ever renders `builtin()` whose values are fixed and grammatical, and any value edit goes through plan + review). The guarded `record-*` write-path (`driver/record.rs`, transition-validating append subcommands that reject an illegal transition; deliberately reopens `Q-24`'s no-write-path stance and must preserve hand-writable JSONL for a binary-less harness; GATE: measured advisory-adoption evidence from Stage 1). And the opt-in authoritative BOUNDARY gate (`validate --workflow --strict` in CI/pre-commit as the hard-enforcement landing gate per ORQ-1; GATE: a measured-low override rate showing the machine models the workflow well). The far corner (calibration closure, a `workflow-viz` read consuming the driver's JSON, multi-repo) stays out of scope until its own scale is real.

YAGNI boundary (what NOT to build). No persisted FSM-state store and no stateful `drive` daemon/REPL (recompute statelessly each call). No judgment in the spec or in `step` (verdicts, risk classes, and human decisions are only ever `Input` variants). No general/Turing-complete process DSL (the spec encodes THIS workflow's fixed constants/sequences/paths/tiers; a new rule KIND is code). No increment-level DAG scheduling (the DAG is step-granularity only). No generating the RATIONALE/role/contract/discipline prose (only the control-constant fragments are generated; the WHY stays hand-authored). No performing isolation (the tool emits the resolved tier; the orchestrator isolates). No per-turn polling during active work (a negative token trade; scope to resume, boundary, and decision points). No flipping to authoritative-blocking before the override rate is measured low. And the driver never subsumes the re-grounding step or Q-50.

### `doc-redundancy-cleanup`: single-source the type-A doc redundancies and fix the type-C accuracy bugs (`Q-49`)

Not started. Decided (`Q-49`, human, 2026-07-19). Cross-document redundancy between the pack docs/prompts and the canonical `pack/AGENTS.md` was raised by the human on Principle 8's one-source-of-truth clause and routed audit-first. Two read-only audits are recorded under `docs/plans/doc-redundancy.explorations/` (`audit-readme-vs-agents.md`, `audit-pack-prompts-vs-agents.md`); they are the detailed evidence. Findings: README-vs-AGENTS has no severe drift (mostly legitimate type-B onboarding summaries, and the cap number is not restated), with one borderline type-C, R-11: `README.md:187` states the W3 convergence rule as an absolute and omits the waiver exemption that `AGENTS.md:139` carries. pack-prompts-vs-AGENTS has 9 type-A real single-source-of-truth drift-risk copies of canonical rules/constants (the bare `five` cap value, the clean-round counts plus the risk-class definition copied verbatim at `orchestrator.md:19`, the four-level severity names restated across ~5 files, the findings-file filename templates and the isolation-tier order restated right beside an existing "see `AGENTS.md`" pointer) plus 1 borderline type-C, C-1: `checks-reviewer.md` and `checks-guidance.md` both restate the treefmt-v2 format-check caveat with no single owner. DECIDED: SINGLE-SOURCE-WITH-REFERENCES. State each canonical rule/constant once in `pack/AGENTS.md` and replace the ~9 type-A pack copies with a pointer to it; leave the type-B role-scoped procedure prose as is; and fix the 2 borderline type-C accuracy bugs. the reference-instead-of-restate approach is already modelled by the `pack/isolation-guidance.md:3` model, which references the tier rule instead of restating it and is the phrasing the type-A fixes should copy. Ranked type-A targets (from the audit's "Highest-value single-source fixes"): (1) A-2 `orchestrator.md:19`, remove the verbatim two-sentence copy of the clean-round counts and the risk-class definition, reference the Convergence rule in `AGENTS.md`; (2) A-1 plus A-8 (`orchestrator.md:19`, `LEDGER.template.md:9`), drop the literal cap value `five` and the one/two clean-round numbers, reference the rule; (3) A-3 plus A-7 (`orchestrator.md:7`, `orchestrator.md:11`), delete the findings-file filename templates and the isolation tier-order restatements that sit right beside an existing "see `AGENTS.md`" reference; (4) A-5 plus A-6 (~5 files), give the four-level severity scale and its "absolute rating, not relative" gloss one canonical statement in `AGENTS.md`, keep the enumeration only in the two producer prompts that assign severities (reviewer, checks-reviewer) and reference it from the consumers (orchestrator, instrument, triager); (5) A-4 (`orchestrator.md:5`, `triager.md:3`), keep the bare do/don't instruction in each prompt but drop the copied separate-triager bias rationale, and A-9 (`orchestrator.md:23`), compress the verbatim acceptance-is-a-single-pass sentence to a reference. Type-C fixes: R-11, add "(or a recorded waiver)" to `README.md:187` so the absolute is no longer stated; C-1, give the treefmt-v2 format-check caveat one home in `pack/checks-guidance.md` (the module's own explanatory doc) and point `pack/prompts/checks-reviewer.md` at it, keeping only the reviewer's own action. Scope is a pack-doc edit (touches `pack/AGENTS.md`, `pack/prompts/*.md`, `pack/LEDGER.template.md`, `pack/checks-guidance.md`, `README.md`), so when built it goes through the normal implementer plus review loop; sequenced AFTER Inc 6 of `structured-skeleton` (done). Chosen over leaving the copies duplicated and over generating the pack from `AGENTS.md` (too heavy for prose).

### `doc-currency-guidance`: documentation currency as core cross-cutting workflow guidance (`Q-50`)

Not started. Decided (`Q-50`, human, 2026-07-19). Make DOCUMENTATION CURRENCY a CORE CROSS-CUTTING guidance in the workflow, not an optional module and not a deterministic check alone. Concretely: add a documentation-impact assessment to the PLANNER's duties (when folding a change, the planner identifies which docs and prompts the change makes stale) and to `pack/AGENTS.md` phase 2, plus a doc-currency check at the ACCEPTANCE step (does the shipped change leave any doc or prompt stale?). Reuse the EXISTING roles rather than adding a new role or module: the planner assesses, the implementer updates, the reviewer verifies. Chosen over (a) an opt-in `--module` bundle and (b) a deterministic check alone. This complements, and is a prerequisite for, the workflow-driver's later doc-generation stages (`Q-51`) if those are built: the driver can generate a doc fragment from a machine definition only once staleness is a first-class assessment the workflow already makes. Scope is a pack-doc-and-guidance edit (touches `pack/AGENTS.md`, the planner prompt, and the acceptance step guidance) that goes through the normal implementer plus review loop; sequenced after Inc 6 of `structured-skeleton` (done).

### `task-entry-regrounding`: stakes-scaled task-entry re-grounding and per-step provenance links (`Q-53`)

Not started. Decided (Socratic, `Q-53`, human, 2026-07-19). Add a workflow step that is a LIGHT, stakes-scaled TASK-ENTRY RE-GROUNDING and human alignment check, run before starting a step and on resume. It is sourced from the DURABLE artifacts (plan Step Detail by slug, ledger RESUME STATE, decision receipts by q_id, findings by path, code by file:line/commit), NOT conversation history: history is ephemeral, harness- and machine-specific, and not VCS-shareable, so a fact found only in history is a durability bug to flush into the artifacts, not to cite; out-of-VCS history pointers were rejected as moving the fragility rather than fixing it. The orchestrator writes a short brief (what the task is, why it exists, the cited evidence including the decision RECEIPT with the human's recorded choice, and what it is about to do) and pushes it for a go/no-go via the human-input contract, scaled to stakes. The step also ADOPTS structured PER-STEP PROVENANCE links (a step -> the decisions, findings, and commits that justify it, mirroring questions' `folded_into`/`receipt`), which `render` can present as "why this step exists". Artifact fidelity is ensured by: capture-at-decision with the human's sign-off (the receipt, with `chosen` validated as a member of `options`, is signed evidence, not a paraphrase); two human-confirmation checkpoints (the fold-in queue-push and this task-entry re-grounding); independent review of a capture against its receipt and the step it changes; structured-over-prose for load-bearing facts (fields cannot paraphrase-drift); and the W4 decided-needs-receipt enforcement (extendable, for example to flag a decided item whose recorded choice is not among its receipt options). Chosen over no-regrounding and over out-of-VCS history pointers. It complements the workflow-driver (`Q-51`), which the driver's design pass found should complement rather than subsume this step. Scope goes through the normal implementer plus review loop; sequenced after Inc 6 of `structured-skeleton` (done).

### `human-input-gate-reinforce`: reinforce the human-input contract at the orchestrator's point of action (`Q-54`)

Not started. Decided (`Q-54`, human, 2026-07-19). The human-input contract (present the viable options with their trade-offs, an explicit recommendation, and reasoning judged against the numbered Project Principles, then let the human decide) is stated once in `pack/AGENTS.md`, but the orchestrator drifted off it after a context compaction: it presented several decision gates as options-with-trade-offs while omitting the recommendation and the Principle-judged reasoning. This is control-drift, an agent forgetting a rule that applies right at the point of action, not a missing rule, so the fix reinforces the rule where it is applied rather than restating it.

Part (a), this step: make the human-input contract a REQUIRED VISIBLE EMITTED ARTIFACT at every human decision gate. Evidence that refined this (found while scoping, 2026-07-19, human-decided): `pack/prompts/orchestrator.md` ALREADY stated the per-gate rule at its point of action ("Every time you put a decision to the human ... present the options, their trade-offs, a recommendation, and reasoning judged against the ... Project Principles"), and the orchestrator drifted off it anyway after a compaction. So a plain prose statement of the rule, even at the point of action, demonstrably did not prevent the drift, and merely strengthening that sentence would be close to a no-op. Instead, change `pack/prompts/orchestrator.md` so that before every human gate the orchestrator must EMIT a structured block (the options, each option's trade-offs, an explicit recommendation, and the reasoning judged against the numbered Project Principles), so that an omission of the recommendation or the Principle-judged reasoning is immediately conspicuous to the human and to a reviewer rather than a silent lapse. It still REFERENCES the canonical human-input contract in `AGENTS.md` rather than restating it (Principle 8, one source of truth); what it adds is a required OUTPUT FORMAT at the point of action, not a new rule. Reasoning judged against the Principles: making the recommendation a required visible artifact is the make-illegal-states-conspicuous analog of Principle 5 (a missing recommendation is self-evident, not merely rule-violating); Principle 6 (evidence first) justifies the stronger form because the plain-prose form is now known to fail; Principle 8 (structure over prose for load-bearing facts) points the same way.
